scores.correctness_notes: Covers the Python client side well (wait_change, get_change, change.ready, change.err) but misses key gold standard details: CLI commands (pebble changes / pebble tasks), REST API endpoints (GET /v1/changes/{id}, /v1/changes/{id}/wait), change-update notices, and state persistence to $PEBBLE/.pebble.state.
scores.hallucination_notes: container.get_pebble() does not appear to be a real public method on the ops Container class — the pebble client is not exposed this way. Minor fabrication, hence score 1.
